content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach makes multiple specific, timely reads that change the direction of the conversation. Turn 4 names the linguistic caveat ('I notice you went straight back to is it okay') — a precise read of the rush-to-permission pattern. Turn 6 names a somatic/vocal shift in real time ('notice what happened in your voice just then. It softened') and distinguishes the said from the shown. Turn 10 names the function of the 'selfish question' as a defensive stopping place ('the place where you stop, so you don't have to feel that one') — a pattern-level read. Turn 12 invites somatic tracking after the inherited-label reveal. Reads consistently precede questions rather than coming as blind inquiry.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Refuses the verdict and pivots to the want, but no explicit read named yet."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the linguistic caveat / permission-seeking move explicitly ('went straight back to is it okay')."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Specific somatic/vocal read in real time — voice softening — distinguishing said from shown."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Question without an explicit read, but follows from the prior read."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pattern-level read: names the 'selfish question' as a defensive stopping point that protects against feeling."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Invites somatic tracking after the inherited-label surfaces."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Names the want vs should distinction; lands the work."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Reads are specific, AoA-flavored (linguistic caveat, vocal softening, defense-as-stopping-place), and consistently precede the questions."
}
